title: Discord Documentation
prompt_source: Inline docs prompt; CONNECTOR_DOCUMENTATION_GUIDELINES fetched via getDocGuidelines()

sections.title: Overview
sections.content:
**Discord** is a chat / voice / community platform exposing a REST API at `https://discord.com/api/v10`. This connector — `DiscordConnector` (`CONNECTOR_TYPE = "discord"`, `AUTH_TYPE = "api_key"`) — wraps the operational surfaces a Shielva tenant typically needs from a Discord bot or OAuth app:

| Surface | Base path | Capability |
|---|---|---|
| Users | `/users/@me`, `/users/{user.id}` | Identify the bot/user, look up other users |
| Guilds | `/users/@me/guilds`, `/guilds/{guild.id}` | List + read guilds (servers) the bot belongs to |
| Channels | `/guilds/{guild.id}/channels`, `/channels/{channel.id}` | List + read text/voice/thread channels |
| Messages | `/channels/{channel.id}/messages` | Send / read / edit / delete channel messages |
| Members | `/guilds/{guild.id}/members` | List + read members |
| Roles | `/guilds/{guild.id}/members/{user.id}/roles/{role.id}` | Add / remove role from member |
| Webhooks | `/channels/{channel.id}/webhooks`, `/webhooks/{webhook.id}/{webhook.token}` | Create + execute server-side webhooks |

The connector normalises guilds + channels + messages into `NormalizedDocument` (id = `f"{tenant_id}_{source_id}"`), surfaces standalone `async def` methods per user-requested operation (OCP), retries 429/5xx with exponential backoff that honours Discord's `retry_after` body field (3 attempts), and never embeds raw HTTP in `connector.py` (SOC — all HTTP delegated to `client/http_client.py::DiscordHTTPClient`).

sections.title: Quick Start
sections.content:
### 1. Mint a Discord bot token

1. Sign in to the **Discord Developer Portal** at https://discord.com/developers/applications.
2. Click **New Application**, name it (e.g. `Shielva Connector`), accept the ToS.
3. In the left nav open **Bot** → click **Reset Token** → confirm. Copy the token immediately (Discord shows it only once).
4. Optional but typical: enable the **Privileged Gateway Intents** you need (Server Members, Message Content) — required for `list_guild_members` and reading message bodies in non-DM channels.
5. Open **OAuth2 → URL Generator**, tick `bot` scope, pick the bot permissions you need (Send Messages, Read Message History, Manage Roles, …), and open the generated URL to invite the bot to your guild(s).

### 2. Install in ACP

In **ACP → Manage Connectors → Discord**, fill in:

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| `bot_token` (secret) | Bot token from step 3 |
| `oauth_token` (secret, optional) | Bearer OAuth2 user-token override |
| `base_url` (text, optional) | Override `https://discord.com/api/v10` (rarely needed) |
| `rate_limit_per_min` (number) | Soft cap, default 50 |

Click **Install**. `install()` validates `bot_token` (or `oauth_token`) is present (it does NOT call the API), initialises the `DiscordHTTPClient`, and returns `ConnectorStatus(HEALTHY, AUTHENTICATED)`.

### 3. First health check

The gateway calls `health_check()` which issues `GET /users/@me` as a lightweight probe. On 2xx → `HEALTHY + CONNECTED`. On 401 → `OFFLINE + TOKEN_EXPIRED`. On 403 → `UNHEALTHY + INVALID_CREDENTIALS`. On 429 → `DEGRADED + CONNECTED` with structured-log warning.

sections.title: Authentication
sections.content:
Discord REST API supports two server-friendly auth schemes; this connector exposes one install field (`bot_token`) and treats it as the api_key (`AUTH_TYPE = "api_key"`).

### Credential model

- `bot_token` is sent as `Authorization: Bot <bot_token>` (Discord-specific prefix).
- `oauth_token` (when set) overrides the header to `Authorization: Bearer <oauth_token>` — useful when the integration needs to act on behalf of a Discord user instead of a bot.
- `User-Agent: DiscordBot (https://shielva.ai, 1.0)` is sent on every request (Discord requires a non-empty UA on REST calls).

### Bot vs OAuth side-by-side

| Aspect | Bot token (default) | OAuth user token (override) |
|---|---|---|
| Header | `Authorization: Bot <token>` | `Authorization: Bearer <token>` |
| Lifetime | Long-lived; rotates only on Reset Token | Short-lived; refresh out of scope for this revision |
| Acts as | Bot user | The end user who granted consent |
| Required scope | `bot` (at invite time) | `identify guilds messages.read` (typical) |
| Install field | `bot_token` (required) | `oauth_token` (optional) |

### Lifecycle

| Phase | Behaviour |
|---|---|
| `install()` | Validates `bot_token` or `oauth_token` is present. Initialises `DiscordHTTPClient`. Does **not** call any Discord endpoint. |
| `authorize(...)` | Returns a synthetic `TokenInfo(access_token=<token>, token_type="Bot"|"Bearer")` — no exchange is performed. |
| `health_check()` | `GET /users/@me` — minimal probe. |
| `ensure_token()` | Not called — bot tokens are long-lived; OAuth refresh is out of scope. |

### Header contract

Every outbound request:

```
Authorization: Bot <bot_token>            ← default
            OR Bearer <oauth_token>        ← when oauth_token is set
User-Agent:    DiscordBot (https://shielva.ai, 1.0)
Content-Type:  application/json
Accept:        application/json
```

sections.title: Configuration
sections.content:
### Install fields (per-tenant)

Read via `self.config.get(...)` in `connector.py::__init__` — never hardcoded.

| Key | Type | Required | Read in code | Purpose |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `bot_token` | secret | yes | `self.bot_token` | Authorization header value (Bot prefix added by HTTP client) |
| `oauth_token` | secret | no | `self.oauth_token` | Optional Bearer override |
| `base_url` | text | no | `self.base_url` (defaults to `https://discord.com/api/v10`) | API base URL |
| `rate_limit_per_min` | number | no | `self.rate_limit_per_min` (default 50) | Client-side soft cap |

### Class constants (provider-wide)

```python
REQUIRED_CONFIG_KEYS = ["bot_token"]
_STATUS_MAP = {
    401: ("OFFLINE",   "TOKEN_EXPIRED"),
    403: ("UNHEALTHY", "INVALID_CREDENTIALS"),
    429: ("DEGRADED",  "CONNECTED"),
}
DISCORD_BASE_URL = "https://discord.com/api/v10"
```

sections.title: API Methods
sections.content: All public async methods on `DiscordConnector`. SOC: connector.py orchestrates only; HTTP lives in `client/http_client.py::DiscordHTTPClient`.

children.title: Lifecycle
children.content:
### `async install() -> ConnectorStatus`
Validate `bot_token` (or `oauth_token`); initialise `DiscordHTTPClient`. Does NOT call the API.

### `async authorize(auth_code, state) -> TokenInfo`
No-op for api_key auth — returns a `TokenInfo` whose access_token is the configured bot/OAuth token.

### `async health_check() -> ConnectorStatus`
Issues `GET /users/@me` as a lightweight probe. Classifies failures by `_STATUS_MAP`.

### `async sync(since=None, full=False, kb_id=None, webhook_url=None) -> SyncResult`
Iterates guilds → channels (text-typed only) → most recent 50 messages → `NormalizedDocument` → `ingest_document`. Returns `SyncResult` with `documents_synced / _failed / _found`.

children.title: Guilds
children.content:
### `async list_guilds(limit=100, before=None, after=None) -> List`
`GET /users/@me/guilds?limit={N}&before={S}&after={S}` — snowflake cursor pagination.

### `async get_guild(guild_id) -> Dict`
`GET /guilds/{guild_id}`.

children.title: Messages
children.content:
### `async send_message(channel_id, content, embeds=None, components=None) -> Dict`
`POST /channels/{channel_id}/messages` — body: `{content, embeds?, components?}`.

### `async get_message(channel_id, message_id) -> Dict`
`GET /channels/{channel_id}/messages/{message_id}`.

### `async list_messages(channel_id, limit=50, before=None, after=None, around=None) -> List`
`GET /channels/{channel_id}/messages?limit={N}` — snowflake cursor pagination.

### `async edit_message(channel_id, message_id, content) -> Dict`
`PATCH /channels/{channel_id}/messages/{message_id}` — body: `{content}`.

### `async delete_message(channel_id, message_id) -> Dict`
`DELETE /channels/{channel_id}/messages/{message_id}`.

children.title: Members · Roles · Users
children.content:
### `async list_guild_members(guild_id, limit=100, after=None) -> List`
`GET /guilds/{guild_id}/members`.

### `async get_user(user_id) -> Dict`
`GET /users/{user_id}`.

### `async add_role(guild_id, user_id, role_id) -> Dict`
`PUT /guilds/{guild_id}/members/{user_id}/roles/{role_id}`.

### `async remove_role(guild_id, user_id, role_id) -> Dict`
`DELETE /guilds/{guild_id}/members/{user_id}/roles/{role_id}`.

children.title: Webhooks
children.content:
### `async create_webhook(channel_id, name) -> Dict`
`POST /channels/{channel_id}/webhooks` — body: `{name}`.

### `async execute_webhook(webhook_id, webhook_token, content, embeds=None) -> Dict`
`POST /webhooks/{webhook_id}/{webhook_token}` — body: `{content, embeds?}`.

sections.title: Error Handling
sections.content:
Exception hierarchy in `exceptions.py`:

```
DiscordError                          # base; carries status_code + response_body
├── DiscordAuthError                  # 401 / 403
├── DiscordBadRequestError            # 400
├── DiscordNotFoundError              # 404
├── DiscordConflictError              # 409
├── DiscordRateLimitError             # 429 — retry_after
└── DiscordServerError                # 5xx
```

Back-compat aliases preserved:
```
DiscordNetworkError = DiscordServerError      # legacy name
DiscordNotFound     = DiscordNotFoundError
DiscordAPIError     = DiscordError
```

### Retry behaviour (`client/http_client.py::_request`)

| Status | Action |
|---|---|
| 400 / 401 / 403 / 404 / 409 | Raise immediately |
| 429 | Sleep for the body's `retry_after` (capped at `_MAX_RETRY_AFTER=10s`), then retry up to `_MAX_RETRIES=3` |
| 5xx | Exponential backoff `_BACKOFF_BASE * 2 ** attempt` (0.5s, 1s, 2s), retry up to 3 |
| `httpx.TimeoutException` / `httpx.NetworkError` | Backoff, retry up to 3, then raise `DiscordNetworkError` |

### How errors surface to the gateway

`health_check()` catches exceptions and maps via `_STATUS_MAP`:

```
401 → ConnectorStatus(OFFLINE,   TOKEN_EXPIRED)
403 → ConnectorStatus(UNHEALTHY, INVALID_CREDENTIALS)
429 → ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED,  CONNECTED)   — logged as warning
other → ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED, CONNECTED)
```

sections.title: Troubleshooting
sections.content:
| Symptom | Probable cause | Fix |
|---|---|---|
| `ConnectorStatus(OFFLINE, MISSING_CREDENTIALS)` at install | `bot_token` and `oauth_token` both blank | Re-enter the bot token in ACP. Developer Portal shows the token only once at generation — Reset Token if lost. |
| `ConnectorStatus(UNHEALTHY, INVALID_CREDENTIALS)` at health_check | 403 from Discord — bot lacks the required intent or guild permission | In the Developer Portal → Bot → enable Server Members / Message Content intents. Re-invite the bot to the guild with the right permissions. |
| `ConnectorStatus(OFFLINE, TOKEN_EXPIRED)` at health_check | 401 — bot token rotated or OAuth token expired | Reset the bot token in the Developer Portal and re-enter, or refresh the OAuth token out-of-band. |
| `discord.http.rate_limited` warnings spike | Hitting 429 (Discord per-route bucket) | The client sleeps `retry_after` (capped at 10s) and retries. Lower request volume or batch fewer per minute. |
| 404 on a channel/message call | `channel_id`/`message_id` wrong, OR bot not in the channel | Verify the snowflake. Verify the bot was invited to the guild with channel-read permission. |
| `ImportError: cannot import name 'DiscordNetworkError'` | Older exceptions.py without back-compat aliases | Already fixed — `DiscordNetworkError = DiscordServerError` alias added in `exceptions.py`. |
| Webhook returns 401 on execute_webhook | Wrong webhook_token | Tokens belong to the specific webhook id; re-fetch via the channel's webhooks list. |
